how_to_run: Per references/evals.md: for each eval, run the prompt with-skill and baseline (no skill) in fresh sessions, in parallel, against disposable copies. Replace <workspace> with a scratch dir. Copy the fixture directory into the prompt's target path; if it contains SKILL.fixture.md, rename that copy to SKILL.md inside the scratch dir only. Grade transcripts against the assertions, not just outputs.

evals.name: fix-nontriggering-description
evals.prompt: my github-images skill basically never fires when i paste a github issue url even tho thats literally what its for. can you work out why and fix it? its at <workspace>/github-images
assertions: Diagnoses the description (not the body) as the trigger surface
Rewrite is third person, states what + when with user-typed trigger contexts, <=1024 chars
Does not summarise the workflow into the description; body not bloated
Validates the change (check.sh) and proposes a trigger re-test including near-miss negatives

evals.name: create-skill-from-scratch
evals.prompt: i want a skill that makes agents write proper git commit messages in my repos - small coherent commits, imperative subject line, body explains why not what, no 'various fixes' rubbish. create it in <workspace>/ - call it whatever makes sense
assertions: check.sh passes: closed frontmatter set, directory name equals name
Description: third person, what + when, includes a negative trigger
Classifies the failure type and matches form to it (recipes + worked examples for shape; rationalisation table for pressure excuses)
Body is lean and timeless; proposes testing before shipping
Applies the mechanism gate (must-run-every-time steps belong in hooks/commands, not a skill)

evals.name: review-before-publishing
evals.prompt: before i publish my hetzner-server skill can you review it and tell me what needs fixing? its at <workspace>/hetzner-server
assertions: Catches volatile facts as standing prose (prices, version literals)
Catches machine-specific paths and unchecked personal-tool dependencies as a publish blocker
Verifies domain claims against the live tool - values as well as flag syntax - and does not claim 'couldn't verify' without a failed command -v
Known miss to watch for (iteration 3): flag syntax verified but argument values not - the planted bad value must be caught
evals.history: Known misses to guard against: no live verification; a false 'no CLI' excuse without command -v; verification of flag syntax but not argument values.

evals.name: threshold-vs-snapshot-discrimination
evals.prompt: review my kafka-consumer skill for anything that'll go stale before i publish it - its at <workspace>/kafka-consumer
assertions: Keeps load-bearing version thresholds: does NOT flag 'since Kafka 3.0' or 'Python 3.12+' as doc-rot
Flags the self-ageing snapshots: the 'Verified against ... checked 2026-06-30' banner, 'Recent changes (2025-2026)', 'renamed ... recently', 'as of mid-2026'
For snapshots, recommends repoint-to-live-source, a timeless reword, or an honest as-of caveat - not bare date deletion that leaves a stale fact reading as current
Runs check.sh and notes its doc-rot warnings fire on the snapshot phrasing but not on the version literals
evals.history: Baseline (pre-reframe wording) over-flags 'since Kafka 3.0' / '3.12+' as version-literal rot and/or recommends bare date deletion; the reframe must fix both without losing the true snapshot catches.
